name: SuperPawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn shop operator with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans, retail sales, and gold buying services.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn shop chain with more than 3,300 retail locations. The company serves cash and credit-constrained consumers across 29 U.S. states and Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador. FirstCash is indexed in both the S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000, employing approximately 22,000 people globally.

FirstCash's core business model centers on pawn lending—providing small non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property—and retail merchandise sales. Customers can pawn items including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments for immediate cash. Beyond pawn loans, the company offers layaway services (with 10% down payments), gold and precious metal buying at spot prices, and retail sales of previously pawned merchandise. Through its subsidiary AFF, FirstCash also operates lease-to-own and retail finance solutions across 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale and geographic reach—operating over 3,300 locations making it the largest pawn operator internationally. The company leverages technology-driven point-of-sale systems and maintains a physical store locator and online inventory system. Their focus on serving underbanked consumers combines collateral-based lending with retail goods sales, creating a dual revenue model. The layaway service provides an alternative to credit for customers seeking structured payment options.

FirstCash is appropriate for consumers facing immediate cash needs who own valuable collateral and prefer non-credit-based lending. However, pawn loans carry implicit costs through the difference between collateral value and loan amount, plus storage and redemption considerations. The company's scale and public market listing indicate operational stability, though consumers should understand pawn loans are fundamentally different from traditional credit products and do not build credit history.
address: 2020 W Craig Rd #200, North Las Vegas, NV 89032

company_info.headquarters: 2020 W Craig Rd #200, North Las Vegas, NV 89032

pros: Largest international pawn operator with 3,300+ locations, ensuring accessibility and standardized processes
No credit check required—loans based solely on collateral value, serving unbanked and credit-constrained consumers
Immediate cash at point of sale for pledged items
Diverse merchandise accepted: jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, instruments
Gold and precious metal buying at competitive spot prices for quick cash conversion
Layaway service with affordable 10% down payment option for budget-conscious shoppers
Lease-to-own solutions through AFF subsidiary across 15,000+ retail partners for alternative financing
cons: Pawn loans do not build credit history, offering no path to improved creditworthiness
High implicit cost—redemption typically requires repaying loan amount plus fees/interest, losing principal collateral value
Risk of losing personal property if loan cannot be repaid within agreed timeframe
Limited to consumers with tangible, valuable assets to pledge as collateral
Storage and retrieval logistics required; items may be resold if redemption deadline passes
best_for: Unbanked or credit-constrained consumers needing emergency cash without credit verification
Individuals with valuable items (jewelry, electronics, instruments) seeking quick liquidity
Customers preferring asset-backed loans over credit products or payday loans
Budget-conscious shoppers using layaway service to afford merchandise through affordable down payments
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for unbanked or credit-constrained consumers facing immediate cash needs who own valuable collateral and want to avoid credit-based lending. The primary caveat is that pawn loans carry high implicit costs through collateral loss and do not build credit history, making them suitable only for temporary liquidity needs rather than long-term financial solutions.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Retail sales of merchandise purchased from pawn transactions
Gold, silver, and platinum buying at spot prices with immediate cash payment
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Lease-to-own financing through AFF subsidiary at 15,000+ retail merchant locations
Retail finance payment solutions for consumer goods and services
Store locator service to find nearest location
Online inventory browsing and merchandise discovery
Non-recourse pawn lending (customer not liable beyond collateral loss)
Merchandise buying service for items of value
Point-of-sale technology systems for loan processing
